text:
There was an old woman who lived at the corner of Maple and Cedar. She loved birds. Every spring she’d listen. And every time a magpie would say nice things.

One year none came. Just nothing. No tweeting. No glittering feathers. Just nothing.

The old woman grew lonely. She got a book. She read about magpies. No magpies ever saved anyone.

With nothing to do, she started knitting. Bluebirds. Ravens. Owls.

Four years. Nothing ever sang back.

One night she dreamed. An owl.

The owl said.

“We live high up. It snows up here.

You love snow. Come visit.”

The owl hooted.

Weeks later she woke up. Had snow.

She went out. There were birds.

They chirped. She sang them songs.

That winter.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
